questions.question: According to the material, most slips at work happen when a floor becomes:
What does the material give as the usual cause of most trips at work?
options: Poor housekeeping, such as clutter and obstructions left in walkways

questions.question: The Health and Safety Executive (HSE) records slips and trips as a leading source of what every year?
Which fracture caused by a fall does the material describe as especially serious?
Why can a 'fear of falling' actually make matters worse for a person?
options: They avoid walking and stairs, so muscles weaken and balance declines, raising the risk of another fall
They quickly regain full strength without help
They automatically become more confident over time
They no longer need any support

questions.question: What does the material describe as the strongest single predictor that a person may fall again?
options: A history of falling in the past year

questions.question: How does NICE guidance describe falls among older people?
options: As always due to medication alone
As multifactorial, with several risk factors usually combining
As caused by a single identifiable factor

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an intrinsic risk factor for falling?
options: A loose rug in the hallway

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an extrinsic risk factor for falling?
A person who has fallen takes warfarin, a blood-thinning medicine. What does the material advise?
options: Give them a hot drink and wait to see how they feel
Help them straight up from the floor by hand
Call 999, because anticoagulants can cause dangerous bleeding after a knock
Assume there is no risk as long as they say they feel fine

questions.question: Which of these is a sign of a possible fracture to look for in someone who has fallen?
options: A shortened or turned-out leg, or an obvious deformity

questions.question: If a person who has fallen is unresponsive or not breathing normally, what should you do?
options: Call 999 and start your emergency procedure
Give them food and drink to help them recover
Wait to see whether they improve on their own
Move them onto a bed before doing anything else

questions.question: What does NICE recommend for older people at risk of falling, as one of the few measures shown to make a real difference?
Equipment such as walking aids and hip protectors should be recommended through assessment, often by:
Every fall must be recorded, even if the person seems unhurt, because a fall without injury is still a fall.
Because a person's falls usually have several causes, tackling just one risk factor on its own is normally enough to prevent them.
If a person may be injured after a fall, you should never lift them from the floor by hand.
Encouraging a person to stay active with safe, regular movement generally increases their risk of falling.
Which of the following are intrinsic risk factors for falling? (Select all that apply)
options: Medication side effects such as drowsiness or dizziness

questions.question: Which of the following are extrinsic (environmental) risk factors for falls? (Select all that apply)
options: A walking frame of the wrong height or left out of reach
An urgent or frequent need for the toilet
Trailing cables, clutter and loose rugs

questions.question: When should you call 999 for a person who has fallen, even if they play it down? (Select all that apply)
options: They hit their head or seem drowsy or confused afterwards
They take blood-thinning medicines such as warfarin or apixaban
They laugh it off and there are no other concerns
They have severe pain and cannot move or bear weight on a limb
They have a minor graze that has already been cleaned

questions.question: What should be recorded after a fall? (Select all that apply)
options: What happened and what care was given
Nothing, provided the person seems unhurt
The date, time and place of the fall
The person's condition, any injury, and who was informed

questions.question: Because falls usually have several causes at once, NICE recommends a ___ assessment that considers all of a person's risk factors together.
If a person may be injured after a fall, you must never lift them from the floor by ___.
NICE recommends ___ and ___ training for older people at risk of falling, as one of the few measures shown to reduce falls.
